description: Mixed-language eval corpus — Swift + TS + Python with intentional cross-language symbol collisions. SB-4 ship-blocker gate from /root/.claude/plans/we-are-about-to-wiggly-lemur.md.

cases.notes: Cross-language collision (no bias). BOTH definitions must surface in top 5. Verifies Swift signals don't unfairly promote OR demote the Swift file relative to the TS one.

Swift-keyword bias via 'enum' (Python uses class). The Swift Logger must rank #1. (rg search uses regex, not AND semantics — multi-token bias must use words that actually co-occur with the symbol's def line.)
Multi-token disambiguation. 'View' alone would surface React's View; adding 'protocol' must promote the Swift protocol definition.
Multi-token query containing the literal 'extension' triggers the +15% extension-target boost. Must surface the Swift extension hits ahead of the React component.
Both Swift and TS Auth definitions must surface in top 5. Single-token query with no bias — neither should dominate by more than 2 ranks. Lower threshold because both are correct answers.
Swift-keyword bias via 'protocol' (TS uses interface). The Swift Auth protocol must rank #1; the TS file is acceptable but not primary.
Garbage query across the mixed corpus. Must return zero results regardless of language.
